subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: Compute $E(X)$ if $X$ has the density function given by (a) $f(x) = \begin{cases} \frac{1}{4}xe^{-x/2} & x > 0 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases}$ (b) $f(x) = \begin{cases} c(1 - x^2) & -1 < x < 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases}$ (c) $f(x) = \begin{cases} \frac{5}{x^2} & x > 5 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases}$
